title: Citrusy Creamy Portuguese Chocolate Dumplings
description: A quick, jain-friendly Portuguese dessert that pairs rich dark chocolate with bright citrus zest in soft, bite-sized dumplings.
instructions: Mix the flour, yeast, salt and brown sugar in a large bowl. Whisk in the warm water until a sticky dough forms. Knead the dough on a lightly floured surface for 3‑4 minutes until smooth.

Cover the dough with a damp towel and let it rise in a warm spot for 30 minutes, or until it nearly doubles in size. While the dough rises, melt the chocolate chips in a heat‑proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, stirring occasionally.
Add the coconut cream, orange zest, lemon zest and maple syrup to the melted chocolate, whisking until the mixture is glossy and smooth. Set aside to cool slightly.

Punch down the risen dough and divide it into 12 equal portions. Flatten each portion into a circle about 4 inches in diameter. Spoon 1 Tbsp of the chocolate filling onto the center of each circle, then fold the dough over to enclose the filling, pinching the edges together to seal.

Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat and add 2 Tbsp of coconut oil. Place the dumplings in the skillet, skin‑side down, and cook for 2‑3 minutes until golden. Flip and cook for another 2‑3 minutes, or until the dough is cooked through.
Serve the dumplings warm, optionally dusted with powdered sugar or a drizzle of extra coconut oil for shine.
Repeat step 5 with the remaining dough portions. Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Reheat a few dumplings in a microwave on 50 % power for 30 seconds, or reheat gently in a skillet.
